LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., HONDA DEVELOPMENT & MANUFACTURING OF AMERICA, LLC'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. THE HONDA ENTITIES' CCP 395(A) MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) THE VENUE IS TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO. IN ADDITION, THE COURT DENIES THE REQUEST OF DEFENDANT FOR PAYMENT OF FEES AND THE MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES SET FOR 1/17/24 IS OFF-CALENDAR PER REQUEST OF THE MOVING PARTY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-10-2024 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-07-2024 Continued To May-08-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-10-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., HONDA DEVELOPMENT & MANUFACTURING OF AMERICA, LLC MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Steve Von Till, Esq. 510-798-1100 Von Till Law firm() for Plaintiff.
Neil Kliebenstein, Esq. and Samuel Galvin, Esq. 408-279-5393 Bowman and Brooke LLP for Defendant AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO. et al.

Hearing is held. Both sides argue. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., HONDA DEVELOPMENT & MANUFACTURING OF AMERICA, LLC'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

The Honda entities' CCP 395(a) motion to change venue is granted. No defendant resides in San Francisco. (Id.; Olson Dec.) The motor vehicle crash did not occur in San Francisco and the victims were not hospitalized in San Francisco. (CCP 395(a).) To the extent witness convenience is now an issue at all, no witness is said to reside in San Francisco. Plaintiffs' "response" filed January 2, 2024 was not authorized by the court.

The venue is transferred to Sacramento. In addition, the Court denies the request of Defendant for payment of fees and the motion to strike punitive damages set for 1/17/24 is off-calendar per request of the moving party.

Prevailing party to prepare a proposed form of order.***

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)

***Order is signed after the hearing, (see scanned order for details)
